lines: @@ -31,6 +32,14 @@ private BigInteger validate(BigInteger y, DHParameters dhParams)
+        // Bound the modulus size before the super-linear legendre/modPow below, so a crafted
+        // oversized p cannot turn key import into a CPU-exhaustion DoS (cf. RSA modulus cap).
+            throw new IllegalArgumentException("DH modulus out of range");
@@ -23,6 +25,14 @@ private BigInteger validate(BigInteger y, DSAParameters params)
+            // Bound the modulus size before the super-linear modPow below, so a crafted oversized
+            // p cannot turn key import into a CPU-exhaustion DoS (cf. RSA modulus cap).
+                throw new IllegalArgumentException("DSA modulus out of range");
+     * Upper bound (in bits) on the prime modulus p accepted when validating an imported
+     * Diffie-Hellman public key. Validation performs a modular exponentiation / Legendre
+     * computation whose cost is super-linear in the size of p, so an unbounded p taken from a
+     * crafted key encoding would turn key import into a CPU-exhaustion denial of service. The
+     * default (16384) is the analogue of {@code org.bouncycastle.rsa.max_size} and is well above
+     * any standardised DH group. Read via {@link #asInteger(String, int)}.
+     * Upper bound (in bits) on the prime modulus p accepted when validating an imported DSA
+     * public key. As with {@link #DH_MAX_SIZE}, validation runs a modular exponentiation whose
+     * cost grows super-linearly in the size of p, so an unbounded p from a crafted encoding is an
+     * import-time CPU-exhaustion vector. Default 16384. Read via {@link #asInteger(String, int)}.
+        // An oversized prime modulus must be rejected at import before the super-linear validation
+        // exponentiation, capping the import-time CPU-exhaustion vector. The value is not prime --
+        // only its bit length matters to the guard, which fires before any modPow/legendre.
+            isTrue("unexpected DH message: " + e.getMessage(), "DH modulus out of range".equals(e.getMessage()));
+        // A normally-sized modulus is still accepted (q == null, so validation returns after the
+        // cheap range check) -- the cap must not reject ordinary keys.
+        // An oversized prime modulus must be rejected at import before the super-linear validation
+        // exponentiation, capping the import-time CPU-exhaustion vector. The value is not prime --
+        // only its bit length matters to the guard, which fires before the modPow.
+            isTrue("unexpected DSA message: " + e.getMessage(), "DSA modulus out of range".equals(e.getMessage()));
